decision: Replace analytics interface-backed metrics with typed metric models and constructor helpers
reason: The scoped analytics DTOs still required runtime type assertions in readers and tests. Typed int64/bool metric wrappers keep the JSON contract explicit while allowing new metric categories to be added through constructors and assembler strategies without modifying existing consumers.
impact: Updated analytics models, the system metrics assembler, and scoped analytics tests to share extensible service/result helpers.

decision: Replace analytics metric interface payloads with typed metric DTOs and centralize test service construction in helper seams.
reason: This keeps the analytics models and targeted tests open for new calculators/options without repeated constructor rewrites or runtime type assertions.
impact: Scoped analytics models/tests now extend through typed wrappers and helper options while preserving existing behavior.
